title: Winter Squash Flatbread
subtitle: with Caramelized Apple and Rosemary
description: Despite the rich flavors, this flatbread is remarkably light. You'll create a creamy "bechamel," or "white sauce," using vegan butter, almond milk, and a squeeze of lemon juice. The squash and apple toppings add a sweet note that offsets the bitter punch of arugula. You may have extra minced rosemary —save it for cooking on your own.

steps.title: ROAST THE SQUASH
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Strip the rosemary leaves from the stems and mince the leaves. Add the squash to a baking sheet with 1 tsp vegetable oil, just 1 tsp minced rosemary, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast until the squash begins to brown in places, about 14 to 18 minutes. Peel and mince the shallot. Peel and mince 1 clove garlic.

steps.title: MAKE THE BECHAMEL
steps.text: Place a small saucepan over medium heat and add the butter. Add the shallot and garlic and cook until softened, about 2 to 3 minutes. Add arrowroot starch and stir into a paste. Add the juice from half the lemon, almond milk, and a pinch of salt. Stir until thickened, about 1 minute, and remove from the heat.

steps.title: CARAMELIZE THE APPLE
steps.text: Thinly slice the apple. Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tsp vegetable oil. Add the sliced apple and 1 tsp of the remaining minced rosemary to the skillet. Sprinkle with salt and cook, stirring frequently, until tender and browned in places, about 4 to 6 minutes. Squeeze in the juice from the remaining lemon onto the apples.

steps.title: CRISP THE NAAN
steps.text: Pop the naan into the oven and heat until it's a little crispy, about 5 to 8 minutes.

steps.title: BUILD THE FLATBREADS
steps.text: Spread the bechamel sauce onto the naan and top with the roasted squash and caramelized apples. Pop the flatbreads back into the oven for 3 minutes to warm. In a medium bowl, toss the arugula with 1 tsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper.

steps.title: CUT AND SERVE
steps.text: Top the winter squash flatbreads with some of the arugula salad. Drizzle with white balsamic vinegar and slice. Serve alongside any remaining arugula salad. Dig in!

tips: *These items will be split between multiple steps.
*Find a head of garlic in the Beetroot Taco recipe bag.
TIP: Toss apples frequently to avoid burning.
TIP: If there are any remaining apples, toss them into the arugula.
